comment: Declares the canonical skills store (~/.agents/skills) this repo wants and how each roster skill is delivered to the three harnesses (Claude, Codex, hermes). Five provenance kinds, and who keeps each fresh: (1) npxTracked{} — the store copy is installed and refreshed by the official npx `skills` CLI from an official upstream (latest from main, no pin). update-skills.sh runs `npx skills add <repo> --skill <name> --agent claude-code --agent codex -g -y` for any absent entry (the multi-agent form lands the real dir in the store ~/.agents/skills/<name> and plants a relative ~/.claude/skills symlink byte-identical to chezmoi's declared form — no ~/.codex dir, since Codex reads the store natively; the hermes profile symlinks are planted by this script, not npx), then `npx skills update -g` refreshes them all. These skills are NOT vendored in chezmoi's dot_agents/skills/. (2) clawhubTracked{} — the store copy is installed and refreshed by the `clawhub` CLI from a ClawHub upstream (the npx lane cannot source ClawHub — `npx skills add` is GitHub-only). Each entry records the exact owner-qualified slug and registry. update-skills.sh installs any absent entry in a throwaway --workdir and moves the CLI's nested @owner/<name> output FLAT into the store as <store>/<name> (its .clawhub/origin.json travels along and pins the owner), then the weekly run refreshes each in place via `clawhub --workdir ~/.agents --dir skills update <name>` — bare store names resolve through origin.json even when several ClawHub users publish the name. Not vendored; like the npx lane, the repo-asserted Codex overlay is re-asserted after every refresh. home-assistant (@iahmadzain) is the runtime-control skill for Claude/Codex ONLY — hermesProfiles [] because hermes has NATIVE Home Assistant runtime tools (ha_list_entities/ha_get_state/ha_list_services/ha_call_service plus a WebSocket state gateway), so the skill would be redundant there; the official homeassistant-ai/skills GitHub repo ships a DIFFERENT skill (home-assistant-best-practices: config-authoring guidance, no runtime device control), tracked separately in npxTracked as its own roster skill. (3) forks{} — vendored copies committed in chezmoi's dot_agents/skills/ and refreshed by `chezmoi apply`, never installed by this script. Each records its upstream so the weekly run drift-checks it and alerts (never modifies) when the upstream moves: `fork: true` marks a deliberately-diverged content fork (moshi, herdr); the rest are vendored because npx cannot install them full-tree (elevenlabs' SKILL.md sits at its repo root beside a scripts/ dir that npx does not copy). lastComparedTreeHash = `git rev-parse HEAD:<skillPath>` (or `<HEAD>^{tree}` when skillPath is `.`) at the last HUMAN comparison; bump it after porting wanted changes by hand. (4) Plain vendored dirs with no forks{} entry — today only tiktok-crawling: a ClawHub-published skill whose store copy stays committed and chezmoi-refreshed because hermes owns its hub copy from ClawHub via hermesRegistry and its hub name differs from the roster name (see the entry note); the former plain-vendored ClawHub dirs home-assistant, sql-toolkit, and summarize-pro moved to clawhubTracked (2026-07-09), so their store copies are installed by the updater, not committed. (5) The cua-driver chezmoi symlink — app-owned content the app maintains; the weekly run refreshes its pack via `cua-driver skills update` (the app's own GitHub-Releases updater — never a write through the symlink). The tiers table classifies every roster skill core|on-demand: it drives the Claude skillOverrides block in private_dot_claude/modify_settings.json and the Codex agents/openai.yaml overlays (committed for vendored skills, re-asserted at run time for npx ones). hermesProfiles is the store->hermes symlink map: it maps each roster skill to the hermes profiles whose skills/ dir carries a store symlink to it ('default' = ~/.hermes/skills, any other name = ~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/skills); [] means the store copy is NOT symlinked into any hermes profile. This map is the FINAL live-truth reach (per-profile enabled set, minus catalog-collision names, minus app-owned): the store copies of collision names (humanizer and the hyperframes router — summarize-pro and todoist-cli were released: their only hermes copies were hub installs, since retired in favor of store symlinks, so no catalog copy wins those names) and of hermes's own registry/official installs are never symlinked into hermes — hermes serves those names from its catalog and its own hub, and a store symlink would shadow them. Fan-out (update-skills.sh and the chezmoi symlink declarations) is driven ENTIRELY by hermesProfiles: non-empty means symlink, [] means do not; there is no separate mechanism kind. hermesRegistry records the skills hermes OWNS from a registry that the weekly hermes phase keeps fresh via `hermes -p <profile> skills update <lockKey>`, keyed by lockKey (a ClawHub slug or skills.sh identifier can differ from the frontmatter name, e.g. tiktok-crawling's hub skill is named tiktok-scraping-yt-dlp, so automation must never key off list names): identifier is the exact install identifier, profiles are the hermes profiles that carry the hub-owned copy — default (Bob) included, walked via `hermes -p default` since its un-entanglement (2026-07-09: kubernetes-specialist, lobster, and todoist-cli moved to pure npx store ownership, so no registry entry has a store-symlinked install path anymore), 'held': true keeps the weekly updater's hands off (none currently held). hermesRegistry and hermesProfiles are DISJOINT by construction: a store-symlinked skill is store-fed and must never be `hermes skills update`d, a hermes-owned skill is hub-fed and never store-symlinked. test/skills-roster-fanout.sh keeps all the tables and the declarations consistent. The superpowersRouting table is the recipe for the hand-patched ~/.hermes/skills/hermes-superpowers mirror (not a roster skill — nothing under it is installed or fanned out): map pairs each legacy superpowers skill name with the hermes-native adaptation that replaces it, and slashCommandsSkill names the dispatcher skill whose SKILL.md must carry the adaptation-map lines. assert-hermes-superpowers-routing.sh re-asserts the mapping from this table (weekly, via update-skills.sh) after anything re-mirrors the tree; its --check mode is the health probe.

hyperframes.note: catalog official install wins the name (collision ruling); the store copy never fans out

tiktok-crawling.note: hub skill's frontmatter name is tiktok-scraping-yt-dlp; update by this lockKey, never the list name. Upstream re-verified live (clawhub.ai/romneyda/skills/tiktok-crawling, v1.0.0); the weekly `hermes -p concerned skills update tiktok-crawling` round-trip was exercised successfully

home-assistant.note: runtime device control for Claude/Codex only — hermesProfiles [] because hermes carries native Home Assistant runtime tools, so the skill would be redundant there; the authoring companion is the separate npx-tracked home-assistant-best-practices

sql-toolkit.note: was hermes-hub-owned in nicodemus (hermesRegistry); now clawhub-updates in the store and reaches nicodemus via a store symlink — the retired hub install is unowned live state to hand-remove

summarize-pro.note: was hermes-hub-owned in default (hermesRegistry) and a HERMES_COLLISION_NAMES entry; the hub install was its ONLY hermes copy (no catalog copy wins the name, exactly like todoist-cli), so it now reaches default via a store symlink — the retired hub install is unowned live state to hand-remove. Store install+update round-trip verified live 2026-07-09

elevenlabs.note: vendored, not a content fork: npx installs only the root SKILL.md and drops the sibling scripts/ dir the skill needs, so the ClawHub full-tree copy stays vendored and this upstream is drift-watched
